data.title: Carl Zeiss Extends Lease at Life Science REIT Facility
data.description:
Life Science REIT tenant Carl Zeiss signed a lease extension at Cambourne Park Science & Technology Campus in Cambourne, UK.

The tenant, occupying 43,300 square feet, extended the lease to 2033 from 2028, the real estate company said Friday.

data.title: India's Sensex Closes Flat at 74332.58
Ensign Energy Services: Current Crude Oil Oversupply Imbalance Reinforcing Producer Customer Discipline >ESI.T
British American Tobacco Reaches Litigation Settlement Deal in Canada
data.description:
British American Tobacco (, BTI.JO) and its Imperial Tobacco Canada subsidiary reached a settlement deal that resolves all their Canadian tobacco litigation after six years of negotiations.

The agreement will be paid using Imperial Tobacco Canada's cash on hand and profits from the future sale of tobacco products in Canada, according to a Friday filing.

British American Tobacco said the settlement follows court-supervised negotiations that had been ongoing since March 2019 after the Canadian business received creditor protection.

Public disclosures under Rule 38 of the Rules must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

Ap37

NOTES ON FORM 38.5(a)

1.             See the definition of "connected principal trader" in Rule 2.2 of Part A of the Rules.

2.             See the definition of "dealing" in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

3.             See the definition of "relevant securities" in Rule 2.1 of Part A of the Rules.

4.             See Rule 2.5(d) of Part A of the Rules.

5.             If the economic exposure to changes in the price of securities is limited, for example, by virtue of a stop loss arrangement relating to a spread bet, full details must be given.

6.             If details included in a disclosure under Rule 38 are incorrect, they should be corrected as soon as practicable in a subsequent disclosure. Such disclosure should state clearly that it corrects details disclosed previously, identify the disclosure or disclosures being corrected, and provide sufficient detail for the reader to understand the nature of the corrections. In the case of any doubt, the Panel should be consulted.

For full details of disclosure requirements, see Rules 8 and 38 of the Rules. If in doubt, consult the Panel.

References in these notes to "the Rules" are to the Irish Takeover Panel Act, 1997, Takeover Rules, 2022.

data.title: India stocks, rupee, swaps, call at 3:30 p.m. IST
data.description:
STOCKS:

The benchmark BSE Sensex was down 0.01%, while the broader NSE index rose 0.03%, as a jump in heavyweight Reliance Industries offset a drop in IT shares.

RUPEE:

The Indian rupee rose 0.28% versus the U.S. dollar to 86.8725, as worries about a slowing U.S. economy and back-and-forth in tariff policies weighed on the dollar this week.

GOVERNMENT BONDS:

The benchmark 10-year bond (IN071034G=CC) was quoted at 102.3175 rupees, with the yield down 2 basis points at 6.7532%, as market participants awaited U.S. economic data to gauge the Federal Reserve's interest rate trajectory, which could also have a bearing on the domestic rate cut cycle.

OVERNIGHT INDEX SWAPS:

The one-year overnight index swap rate (INR1YMIBROIS=CC) was down 1 bps at 6.19%, while the five-year swap rate (INR5YMIBROIS=CC) was unchanged at 5.965%.

CALL MONEY:

India's overnight call money rate (INROND=) was at 5.75%, and the overnight TREPS rate (INTREPTOT=TCCL) was at 6.15%.
